ticket_summary.rough_intent: Figure out whether the current workspace can safely be used for AMOF intake work.
ticket_summary.bounded_goal: Classify the current dirty workspace, identify which truth domains are trustworthy, and prepare a read-only executor prompt for a bounded follow-up.

inspection_scope.notes: Root workspace is a wrapper/config/audit shell, not canonical implementation truth.

source_truth.summary: Repo-role guidance exists and says root is not canonical AMOF implementation truth.

evidence.summary: Declares root as wrapper_config_audit_shell and points AMOF backend/CLI/runtime truth to repos/amof.
Requires separate source, runtime, and workspace truth before execution.

runtime_truth.summary: No fresh runtime evidence was inspected for this workspace-only intake task.

workspace_truth.summary: Workspace state is materially dirty and must be treated as an input to planning, not ignored.

evidence.summary: Visible modified and untracked files indicate that root workspace state can affect task classification.
Ignored external repo checkouts mean root dirt cannot be assumed to represent canonical implementation changes.

allowed_mutations: prepare a follow-up prompt scoped to wrapper/config/audit surfaces only

validation_gates.name: canonical_repo_resolution
validation_gates.requirement: Confirm whether the requested follow-up work belongs in root or in repos/amof before any file edits are allowed.

validation_gates.name: dirty_state_classification
validation_gates.requirement: Identify which dirty files are in-scope versus unrelated before handing work to an executor.

ambiguities.question: Should the follow-up treat current untracked root files as canonical new evidence, archive candidates, or unrelated operator material?
ambiguities.impact: Execution scope cannot be bounded safely until the dirty state is classified.

stop_conditions: Stop if the requested follow-up requires editing files before canonical repo ownership is established.
Stop if dirty workspace material cannot be separated into in-scope and unrelated changes.

reasons: Dirty workspace state can cause the executor to touch unrelated files.
Root workspace is not canonical implementation truth for AMOF runtime changes.

next_executor_prompt: Perform a read-only classification of the current workspace dirt. Determine which files belong to root wrapper/config/audit scope versus canonical repos such as repos/amof. Do not edit files, build, deploy, or push. Return a bounded in-scope file set, any ambiguities that require operator resolution, and a recommendation for whether a follow-up task belongs in root or repos/amof.
